prompt: Tight macro three-quarter detail of a finished coffered ceiling intersection — three molding profiles converge at one corner: a stained red oak crossbeam, a stacked white-painted crown molding, and a recessed panel-mold profile inside the coffer pocket. The miter joint where two crown pieces meet is razor clean, painter's caulk smoothed in the seam. Visible texture: faint brush stroke on the painted crown, real wood grain in the oak, fine sawdust caught in a few molding ridges. A pencil mark and a small finish-nail head visible on the oak beam. Shot with a 100mm macro lens at three-quarter angle, very shallow depth of field — the miter joint tack-sharp, the surrounding ceiling softly defocused. Single warm tungsten work-lamp from camera-left rakes across every molding profile, deep shadow falloff to the right emphasises every shadow line and reveal. Slightly desaturated palette, a small red carpenter pencil resting on the oak beam is the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
